description_long: This is a traditional Chinese siheyuan (quadrangle courtyard house) compound featuring a fully enclosed gray-brick perimeter wall and multiple inward-facing one-story buildings arranged around a central open courtyard. The architecture uses dark gray tiled pitched roofs with extended eaves, and red-painted wooden doors and lattice windows that strongly emphasize a classical Beijing courtyard aesthetic. Inside the compound, the courtyard is primarily stone-paved with planted areas and several trees, and the main hall sits on the central axis facing the courtyard. A prominent entrance gate with red doors and stone steps connects the exterior to the interior yard. Overall, the asset represents a walled residential compound with symmetrical, courtyard-centric layout and traditional masonry-and-wood construction.
description: A traditional Chinese siheyuan courtyard house compound with gray-brick walls, dark tiled roofs, and red doors/windows surrounding a stone-paved central courtyard with trees.
description_view_0: The front perimeter wall is shown with a prominent red entrance gate on the right, with a partial view into the inner courtyard and the main hall beyond.
description_view_1: A side perspective shows the long courtyard-facing wing with repeated red lattice windows, tiled roofs, and the inner yard with greenery.
description_view_2: The rear exterior wall is visible with minimal openings; only the upper rooflines and courtyard trees are seen above the enclosure.
description_view_3: The opposite side view reveals the main hall and adjacent wings facing the courtyard, highlighting red doors/windows, tiled roofs, and trees inside the compound.
attribute_window_arrangement: courtyard-facing lattice windows arranged in repeated bays along the wings, with a mostly symmetrical axial layout
attribute_entryway_design: walled entrance with a recessed red gate and stone steps leading into the central courtyard
